recommendedNextMilestone: M637F Manual Reload QA After Bridge WebSocket Follow-up Fix
manualQaSteps: Start the bridge: dotnet run --project src/OneBrain.ChromeLab.Bridge and confirm console shows 'listening on http://127.0.0.1:8787'
Confirm http://127.0.0.1:8787/runtime returns 200 ok=true BEFORE loading the extension
Load/reload the unpacked extension from browser-extension/onebrain-chrome-lab/
Open the side panel and the Runtime tab
Open DevTools (service worker) Console AND Network panel; keep them open during the test
Observe: connection should reach 'connected' and stay there (no connecting/error/reconnecting cycle)
If it still cycles: capture the WebSocket close code from DevTools Network (1006 vs 1008) and any Console errors, and confirm whether the bridge process was actually running
Verify /debug shows connectedCount=1 and the extension.hello event
ifStillFailingCaptureForM637F: WebSocket close code (1006 transport vs 1008 policy)
Whether HTTP 101 Switching Protocols was reached
risksPending: Final confirmation requires manual reload QA WITH the bridge confirmed running and DevTools console/network captured
If the bridge was simply not running during M637D, the race fix improves robustness but the user must start the bridge
